schema.name: id
schema.description: The DataCite DOI (Digital Object Identifier) string.

schema.name: type
schema.description: The type of the entity (e.g., "dois").

schema.name: attributes
schema.description: The container for all metadata attributes associated with the DOI.

fields.name: alternateIdentifiers
fields.description: An identifier or identifiers other than the primary Identifier (DOI) applied to the resource being registered (e.g., ISSN, ISBN).

fields.name: alternateIdentifier
fields.description: The value of the AlternateIdentifier.

fields.name: alternateIdentifierType
fields.description: The type of the AlternateIdentifier (e.g., ISBN, ISSN, ARK).

fields.name: citationCount
fields.description: The number of citations for this resource as tracked by DataCite Event Data.

fields.name: citationsOverTime
fields.description: A breakdown of citation counts grouped by year.

fields.name: total
fields.description: The total number of citations for the specific year.

fields.name: year
fields.description: The year of the citation count.

fields.name: container
fields.description: Descriptive information about the container (e.g., journal, book) if the resource is part of a larger work.

fields.name: firstPage
fields.description: The first page of the resource within the container.

fields.name: identifier
fields.description: The unique identifier for the container (e.g., the ISSN of the journal).

fields.name: identifierType
fields.description: The type of identifier used for the container (e.g., ISSN, ISBN).

fields.name: issue
fields.description: The issue number of the container.

fields.name: lastPage
fields.description: The last page of the resource within the container.

fields.name: title
fields.description: The title of the container (e.g., Journal Name).

fields.name: type
fields.description: The type of the container (e.g., Journal, Series).

fields.name: volume
fields.description: The volume number of the container.

fields.name: contentUrl
fields.description: The direct URL to the content file (if different from the landing page URL).

fields.name: contributors
fields.description: The institution or person responsible for collecting, managing, distributing, or otherwise contributing to the development of the resource.

fields.name: affiliation
fields.description: The organizational or institutional affiliation of the contributor.

fields.name: affiliationIdentifier
fields.description: The unique identifier of the affiliation (e.g., ROR ID).

fields.name: affiliationIdentifierScheme

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the affiliation.

fields.name: schemeUri
fields.description: The URI of the affiliation identifier scheme.

fields.name: contributorType
fields.description: The type of contributor (e.g., ContactPerson, DataCollector).

fields.name: familyName
fields.description: The surname or family name of the contributor.

fields.name: givenName
fields.description: The personal or first name of the contributor.

fields.name: lang

fields.name: name
fields.description: The full name of the contributor.

fields.description: Information about financial support (funding) for the resource being registered.

fields.description: The code assigned by the funder to the grant or award.

fields.name: geoLocations
fields.description: Spatial region or named place where the data was gathered or about which the data is focused.

fields.name: geoLocationBox
fields.description: A rectangular bounding box definition.

fields.name: eastBoundLongitude
fields.description: The easternmost longitude of the box.

fields.name: northBoundLatitude
fields.description: The northernmost latitude of the box.

fields.name: southBoundLatitude
fields.description: The southernmost latitude of the box.

fields.name: westBoundLongitude
fields.description: The westernmost longitude of the box.

fields.name: geoLocationPlace
fields.description: A description of a geographic location (e.g., city name).

fields.name: geoLocationPoint
fields.description: A specific point location defined by latitude and longitude.

fields.name: pointLatitude
fields.description: The latitude of the point.

fields.name: pointLongitude

fields.name: identifiers
fields.description: A list of identifiers associated with the resource (API field).

fields.name: identifier
fields.description: The value of the identifier.

fields.name: identifierType
fields.description: The type of the identifier.

fields.name: isActive
fields.description: Indicates if the DOI is currently in an active state (Findable).

fields.name: language
fields.description: The primary language of the resource (RFC 5646 codes).

fields.name: metadataVersion
fields.description: The version of the metadata record in the DataCite system.

fields.name: partCount
fields.description: The number of parts associated with this resource.

fields.name: partOfCount
fields.description: The number of collections this resource is a part of.

fields.name: prefix
fields.description: The DOI prefix (e.g., 10.1234) indicating the allocating member.

fields.name: publicationYear
fields.description: The year when the data was or will be made publicly available.

fields.name: published
fields.description: The publication year or date (API display field).

fields.name: publisher
fields.description: The entity that holds, archives, publishes, prints, distributes, releases, issues, or produces the resource. This property is used to formulate the citation.

fields.name: lang
fields.description: The language of the publisher name.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the publisher.

fields.name: publisherIdentifier
fields.description: The unique identifier for the publisher (e.g., ROR).

fields.name: publisherIdentifierScheme
fields.description: The name of the scheme used for the publisher identifier.

fields.description: The URI of the publisher identifier scheme.

fields.name: reason
fields.description: The reason for a state change (e.g., why a DOI was hidden).

fields.name: referenceCount
fields.description: The number of references cited by this resource.

fields.name: registered
fields.description: The timestamp when the DOI was registered.

fields.name: relatedIdentifiers
fields.description: Identifiers of related resources. These must be globally unique identifiers.

schema.name: orcid_identifier
schema.description: All ORCID iDs expressed in a record use the same elements: uri, which is the full URI to the record; path, which is the 16-digit ORCID identifier; and host, which is the domain the record is on.

fields.name: uri

fields.name: path
fields.description: ORCID path (16-character identifier) of the ORCID iD.

fields.name: host
fields.description: ORCID host (base URL) of the ORCID iD.

schema.name: preferences
schema.description: The preferences section records the language the user selected when viewing their ORCID record. The languages are listed in https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes, with the exception of Chinese: Simplified is zh_CN, and traditional is zh_TW.

schema.name: history
schema.description: The history section has information about the creation and current status of the record:

fields.name: creation_method
fields.description: How the record was created.

fields.name: submission_date
fields.description: The date the record was created by the user if the creation method is Direct, Website, or Member-referred, or if the creation method is API; it is the date the researcher claimed the record

fields.name: last_modified_date
fields.description: All items on the ORCID record include metadata about when they were created and when they were modified. These dates are hierarchical; the create date for the section is set to when an item was first added to that section, and the last modified date of sections and groups will match the latest last modified date of any item within the section or group. Created and last modified dates will be returned even if the information they refer to is private. The date is presented as year, month, day, followed by the time given in UCT. Information which has not been edited since it was created will have the same create date and last modified date.

fields.name: visibility
fields.description: Visibility indicates the privacy setting of the item, based on the user’s set visibility preference. If a visibility tag is included when information is posted via the API, it will be ignored. Instead the information will be posted per the user’s default visibility preference as set in their account preferences. Public items can be read by anyone via the API and are shown on the researcher’s public record, Trusted party items are only returned via the API to clients who have read-limited access; they are not on the researcher’s public record. Private items are visible only to the researcher and thesource of the item – the only private items returned via the API are those which were added to the record via your integration.

Like all identifiers, the personal external identifier is described using the tags: type, value, url, and relationship. However, personal identifiers should always have the relationship set to 'self.' The researcher cannot add external identifiers to the record manually, they must be added by a client integration using the API. In this example, the researcher has not sorted the identifiers added to their record, so the display index on both is '0.'

fields.name: educations
fields.description: The Education section records the educational institution(s) where the researcher has studied. It uses the same fields as the Employment section: department-name and role-title for specific information about the researcher’s field(s) of study and the degree(s) awarded; the common date tags to identify when the researcher was affiliated with this organization; and the organization tags to identify the educational institution. We recommend using the highest level organization when adding an education item (i.e. not listing the organization at the department level) and always including the disambiguated organization information.

fields.name: identifiers
fields.description: Identifiers for items on the ORCID record are recorded using the common external-ids tag, this includes identifiers in the person, works, funding, and peer review sections. The identifier is described in four fields: * External id type is required and records the type of identifier (identifiers in the works, funding, and peer review sections must a supported identifiers (https://pub.orcid.org/v3.0/identifiers) * External id value is required and contains the identifier itself External id URL is optional and contains a link the identifier will resolve to External id relationship is required and indicates if the identifier refers to the item itself (such as aDOI for an article or a person identifier) or is the identifier is for a whole that the item is a part of (such as an ISSN for a journal article, or an ISBN for a book chapter); part of identifiers are not used when grouping items. Identifiers can also be for alternate versions of the work, and can be grouped with self and version of identifiers

The identifier is described in four fields: * External id type is required and records the type of identifier (identifiers in the works, funding, and peer review sections must a supported identifiers (https://pub.orcid.org/v3.0/identifiers) * External id value is required and contains the identifier itself External id URL is optional and contains a link the identifier will resolve to External id relationship is required and indicates if the identifier refers to the item itself (such as aDOI for an article or a person identifier) or is the identifier is for a whole that the item is a part of (such as an ISSN for a journal article, or an ISBN for a book chapter); part of identifiers are not used when grouping items. Identifiers can also be for alternate versions of the work, and can be grouped with self and version of identifiers

fields.name: last_modified_date
fields.description: All items on the ORCID record include metadata about when they were created and when they were modified. These dates are hierarchical; the create date for the section is set to when an item was first added to that section, and the last modified date of sections and groups will match the latest last modified date of any item within the section or group. Created and last modified dates will be returned even if the information they refer to is private. The date is presented as year, month, day, followed by the time given in UCT. Information which has not been edited since it was created will have the same create date and last modified date.

fields.name: visibility
fields.description: Visibility indicates the privacy setting of the item, based on the user’s set visibility preference. If a visibility tag is included when information is posted via the API, it will be ignored. Instead the information will be posted per the user’s default visibility preference as set in their account preferences. Public items can be read by anyone via the API and are shown on the researcher’s public record, Trusted party items are only returned via the API to clients who have read-limited access; they are not on the researcher’s public record. Private items are visible only to the researcher and thesource of the item – the only private items returned via the API are those which were added to the record via your integration.

fields.name: educations
fields.description: The Education section records the educational institution(s) where the researcher has studied. It uses the same fields as the Employment section: department-name and role-title for specific information about the researcher’s field(s) of study and the degree(s) awarded; the common date tags to identify when the researcher was affiliated with this organization; and the organization tags to identify the educational institution. We recommend using the highest level organization when adding an education item (i.e. not listing the organization at the department level) and always including the disambiguated organization information.

fields.name: identifiers
fields.description: Identifiers for items on the ORCID record are recorded using the common external-ids tag, this includes identifiers in the person, works, funding, and peer review sections. The identifier is described in four fields: * External id type is required and records the type of identifier (identifiers in the works, funding, and peer review sections must a supported identifiers (https://pub.orcid.org/v3.0/identifiers) * External id value is required and contains the identifier itself External id URL is optional and contains a link the identifier will resolve to External id relationship is required and indicates if the identifier refers to the item itself (such as aDOI for an article or a person identifier) or is the identifier is for a whole that the item is a part of (such as an ISSN for a journal article, or an ISBN for a book chapter); part of identifiers are not used when grouping items. Identifiers can also be for alternate versions of the work, and can be grouped with self and version of identifiers

The identifier is described in four fields: * External id type is required and records the type of identifier (identifiers in the works, funding, and peer review sections must a supported identifiers (https://pub.orcid.org/v3.0/identifiers) * External id value is required and contains the identifier itself External id URL is optional and contains a link the identifier will resolve to External id relationship is required and indicates if the identifier refers to the item itself (such as aDOI for an article or a person identifier) or is the identifier is for a whole that the item is a part of (such as an ISSN for a journal article, or an ISBN for a book chapter); part of identifiers are not used when grouping items. Identifiers can also be for alternate versions of the work, and can be grouped with self and version of identifiers

If a visibility tag is included when information is posted via the API, it will be ignored. Public items can be read by anyone via the API and are shown on the researcher’s public record, Trusted party items are only returned via the API to clients who have read-limited access; they are not on the researcher’s public record. fields.name: educations
fields.description: The Education section records the educational institution(s) where the researcher has studied. It uses the same fields as the Employment section: department-name and role-title for specific information about the researcher’s field(s) of study and the degree(s) awarded; the common date tags to identify when the researcher was affiliated with this organization; and the organization tags to identify the educational institution. We recommend using the highest level organization when adding an education item (i.e. not listing the organization at the department level) and always including the disambiguated organization information.

fields.name: identifiers
fields.description: Identifiers for items on the ORCID record are recorded using the common external-ids tag, this includes identifiers in the person, works, funding, and peer review sections. The identifier is described in four fields: * External id type is required and records the type of identifier (identifiers in the works, funding, and peer review sections must a supported identifiers (https://pub.orcid.org/v3.0/identifiers) * External id value is required and contains the identifier itself External id URL is optional and contains a link the identifier will resolve to External id relationship is required and indicates if the identifier refers to the item itself (such as aDOI for an article or a person identifier) or is the identifier is for a whole that the item is a part of (such as an ISSN for a journal article, or an ISBN for a book chapter); part of identifiers are not used when grouping items. Identifiers can also be for alternate versions of the work, and can be grouped with self and version of identifiers

fields.name: identifiers
fields.description: Identifiers for items on the ORCID record are recorded using the common external-ids tag, this includes identifiers in the person, works, funding, and peer review sections. The identifier is described in four fields: * External id type is required and records the type of identifier (identifiers in the works, funding, and peer review sections must a supported identifiers (https://pub.orcid.org/v3.0/identifiers) * External id value is required and contains the identifier itself External id URL is optional and contains a link the identifier will resolve to External id relationship is required and indicates if the identifier refers to the item itself (such as aDOI for an article or a person identifier) or is the identifier is for a whole that the item is a part of (such as an ISSN for a journal article, or an ISBN for a book chapter); part of identifiers are not used when grouping items. Identifiers can also be for alternate versions of the work, and can be grouped with self and version of identifiers
